title: gagea minima
intro: Gagea minima, also known as the Least Adder's Tongue, is a small, perennial plant species belonging to the lily family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving symptoms of indigestion, improving appetite, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. Gagea minima is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in herbal supplements and tinctures.
